purpose: Focused follow-up TODO after review of the current Autopilot implementation. This file covers the remaining runtime blockers after replacing propose stubs with real strategies: ExecutableProposal validation, strategy-chain fallback semantics, LLM-required new-project behavior, WorkerStrategy real delegation, and E2E/regression tests.

context.source_review: Manual code review of the current main branch after introduction of propose_strategy_registry, ToolCallingLLMStrategy, JsonSchemaLLMStrategy, FlexibleLLMNormalizationStrategy and ModelInvocationService.
context.high_level_result: The architecture moved in the right direction. StubStrategy is no longer the default registration path, and LLM calls are now represented by real strategy classes. However, several runtime-critical defects remain and must be fixed before the new-project flow can be called correct or stable.
key_positive_findings: TaskScopedExecutionService.propose_task_step now uses build_strategy_registry() instead of manually wiring StubStrategy for all non-deterministic strategies.

propose_strategy_registry.py registers real strategy classes for deterministic_handler, worker_strategy, tool_calling_llm, json_schema_llm, flexible_llm_normalization, advisory_proposal and human_review.
ModelInvocationService supports OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions and contains special handling for Ollama URLs.
new_software_project policy is LLM-first in strategy_order.
critical_negative_findings: execute_task_step still calls ExecutableProposal.model_validate(proposal), but ExecutableProposal is currently a dataclass, not a Pydantic model. This is a runtime blocker.

ProposeStrategyOrchestrator still iterates over strategy_order[:max_strategy_attempts]. This cuts off the fallback chain and currently prevents flexible_llm_normalization, worker_strategy, deterministic_handler and human_review from being reached for new_software_project when max_strategy_attempts=2.
WorkerStrategy is still largely dummy logic: workers=[] and runtime_targets=[] are hardcoded, and real worker delegation is TODO.

LLM-required new-project behavior is not strict enough: provider failures currently return declined and may fall through depending on strategy slicing/chain behavior instead of producing an explicit llm_required_but_unavailable terminal outcome when policy requires an LLM.
Critical tests are still missing or not found: execute proposal validation, strategy registry, Orchestrator fallback semantics, LLM-required new-project and E2E Fibonacci project creation.

target_runtime_flow: Planner creates at least one new_software_project/coding task.
For task_kind=new_software_project the default policy requires a real LLM attempt through local-first provider preference such as Ollama/OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
ProposeStrategyOrchestrator evaluates the complete configured strategy chain, not only the first N strategy ids.
ToolCallingLLMStrategy tries real tool calling when tools are available and provider supports it.
JsonSchemaLLMStrategy tries structured JSON when tool calling is unsupported or returns no executable tool calls.
FlexibleLLMNormalizationStrategy tries plain model output and normalizes fenced JSON, strict JSON, file blocks, shell blocks or diffs according to policy.

If policy marks LLM as required and all LLM strategies are unavailable, result becomes needs_review or failed with reason llm_required_but_unavailable. It must not silently produce a fake deterministic success.
Only a valid ExecutableProposal is persisted as last_proposal.
execute_task_step loads last_proposal using the same contract validation used by propose, without dataclass/Pydantic mismatch.
Execution writes real files or performs real tool calls.
Artifact collection and verification decide task completion, not final model prose.
global_acceptance_criteria: No call to ExecutableProposal.model_validate remains unless ExecutableProposal is converted to a Pydantic BaseModel.
The orchestrator never truncates the strategy chain via strategy_order[:max_strategy_attempts].
new_software_project default path attempts a real configured LLM provider before any deterministic/template fallback.
If LLM is required but unavailable, the outcome is explicit and auditable: llm_required_but_unavailable.
Flexible normalization is reachable after tool-calling and JSON-schema strategies fail or decline.
WorkerStrategy either delegates to real workers or clearly returns declined without dummy generated output.
E2E test proves ananta new-project Fibonacci can go propose -> execute -> artifacts -> verification without sgpt.
Regression tests prove prose/invalid JSON cannot be executed and cannot cause infinite retry loops.

tasks.title: Fix ExecutableProposal validation contract in execute_task_step
tasks.problem: execute_task_step still calls ExecutableProposal.model_validate(proposal), but ExecutableProposal is a dataclass. This causes a runtime failure when executing from last_proposal.

implementation_options.name: Stay with dataclasses
steps: Catch ValueError/TypeError instead of only pydantic.ValidationError.

implementation_options.name: Move to Pydantic
steps: Convert ExecutableProposal and ProposeStrategyResult to Pydantic BaseModel.
Keep to_dict() compatibility or replace callers with model_dump().
Keep invariant: command or non-empty tool_calls required.

tasks.recommended_option: A for minimal invasive fix, B later if schema validation becomes more central.
acceptance_criteria: execute_task_step can execute a persisted last_proposal dict without AttributeError.
Invalid proposal without command/tool_calls returns denied invalid_executable_proposal_format.
AdvisoryProposalArtifact, PatchProposalArtifact and FileProposalArtifact are rejected by execute unless explicitly converted/applied by controlled code.
Tests cover command proposal, tool_calls proposal, missing proposal, invalid proposal and advisory proposal.
done_definition: No runtime path calls model_validate on a dataclass.
All proposal validation errors are mapped to deterministic API responses.
Regression test fails before fix and passes after fix.

tasks.title: Fix ProposeStrategyOrchestrator strategy-chain semantics
tasks.problem: The orchestrator currently uses strategy_order[:max_strategy_attempts]. That treats max_strategy_attempts as a limit on number of strategy ids, not attempts. For new_software_project with max_strategy_attempts=2 only tool_calling_llm and json_schema_llm are tried; flexible_llm_normalization and human_review are skipped.
implementation_steps: Replace for strategy_id in strategy_order[:self.policy.max_strategy_attempts] with iteration over the full effective strategy_order.
Rename max_strategy_attempts to max_attempts_per_strategy or deprecate it clearly.
If retries per strategy are needed, nest a retry loop inside each strategy id, not around strategy ids.
Keep attempted_strategies metadata for every attempted strategy.
Record skipped strategies only when policy explicitly disables them, not because of a slicing bug.
Make terminal statuses explicit: executable stops, failed/policy_denied/needs_review stop, declined/advisory continue or stop based on policy.
acceptance_criteria: A policy with seven strategies attempts all seven when each returns declined and on_all_strategies_declined=needs_review.
new_software_project reaches flexible_llm_normalization when tool_calling_llm and json_schema_llm decline.
attempted_strategies contains the complete actual chain with status and reason per strategy.
selected_strategy is set only for executable or terminal selected result.
max_attempts_per_strategy, if present, controls retry count per strategy, not chain length.
done_definition: No strategy_order slicing remains in orchestrator.
Tests prove fallback chain is complete.
Policy naming no longer misleads future implementation.

tasks.title: Enforce LLM-required default for new_software_project
tasks.problem: new_software_project is LLM-first, but provider-unavailable cases are currently modeled as declined inside each LLM strategy. The system needs explicit LLM-required behavior so project creation cannot silently bypass the required LLM path.
implementation_steps: Add policy field llm_required: bool or derive it from llm_mode=primary_with_guardrails for new_software_project.
Add strategy category metadata: llm_strategies=[tool_calling_llm,json_schema_llm,flexible_llm_normalization].
If all configured LLM strategies return llm_required_but_unavailable/provider_unavailable and llm_required=true, stop with needs_review or failed according to policy.
Do not fall back to deterministic_handler or template generation when the LLM was required but unavailable.
Allow explicit admin override for offline/demo mode if deterministic fallback is desired.
Make reason_codes machine-readable: llm_required, llm_provider_unavailable, no_llm_fallback_allowed.
acceptance_criteria: new_software_project with configured Ollama reachable attempts Ollama/OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
new_software_project with no reachable LLM returns needs_review or failed with llm_required_but_unavailable.
new_software_project does not silently continue to deterministic_handler after all LLM strategies fail due to provider unavailability.
coding tasks may still use deterministic_handler first if policy says so, because coding is not necessarily identical to project bootstrap.
Tests cover reachable LLM, unavailable LLM, unsupported tools with JSON fallback, and explicit admin offline fallback.
done_definition: Default new-project behavior really asks an LLM.
The failure mode is explicit and auditable.
No fake deterministic success hides a missing model provider.

tasks.title: Harden ToolCallingLLMStrategy and JsonSchemaLLMStrategy provider behavior
tasks.problem: The real strategy classes exist, but they need stricter provider handling, better prompt/context preparation, and explicit fallback semantics for unsupported tool-calling or schema formats.
implementation_steps: Pass task_kind, goal_id, task_id, expected artifacts, allowed tools and constraints into the LLM prompt/system prompt.
Normalize provider-specific tool call shapes before constructing ExecutableProposal.
Distinguish tools_not_supported from provider_unavailable and model_returned_no_tool_calls.
For Ollama, verify whether the selected local model supports tool calling; if not, decline with tools_not_supported so JsonSchemaLLMStrategy can run.
For JSON schema, use response_format/json_object as fallback but validate the returned JSON against local schema manually.
Never accept raw command/tool_calls without command/tool policy validation.
acceptance_criteria: ToolCallingLLMStrategy returns executable only for validated tool_calls.
ToolCallingLLMStrategy returns declined tools_not_supported when provider/model cannot do tools but provider is otherwise reachable.
JsonSchemaLLMStrategy returns executable for valid command or tool_calls JSON.
JsonSchemaLLMStrategy returns advisory for parse failure, not executable.
Provider 4xx/5xx/timeout/connection errors become LLMUnavailableError-derived results with reason codes.
Tests use mocked HTTP responses and do not require a real Ollama server.
done_definition: LLM strategy behavior is deterministic under mocked provider responses.
Fallback from tools to JSON schema is tested.
No unsafe raw model text becomes executable.

tasks.title: Make FlexibleLLMNormalizationStrategy policy-aware and reachable
tasks.problem: FlexibleLLMNormalizationStrategy exists, but it only works if the orchestrator reaches it. The normalizer also needs policy-aware safety checks before shell/file/diff output can become executable or applicable.
implementation_steps: Ensure orchestrator can reach flexible_llm_normalization after tool_calling_llm/json_schema_llm decline.
Extend LLMResponseNormalizer.normalize(...) signature to accept policy, allowed_tools and command policy context.
Only convert shell blocks into command proposals when policy allows shell execution and command risk policy accepts it.
Convert unified diffs into PatchProposalArtifact, never direct executable command.
Convert file blocks into FileProposalArtifact or safe write_file tool_calls only when write_file is allowed.
Reject absolute paths, path traversal and unsafe file targets.
acceptance_criteria: Fenced JSON with command/tool_calls normalizes to ExecutableProposal after validation.
Shell block becomes executable only with explicit allow_shell policy.
Unified diff becomes PatchProposalArtifact/advisory path, not direct execution.
Plain prose becomes advisory only.
Unsafe paths are rejected with reason code unsafe_path.
Tests cover JSON, shell, diff, file block, prose and malformed output.
done_definition: Flexible normalization is usable as safe LLM fallback.
No raw markdown/prose path can bypass execution policy.
All major LLM response shapes are regression-tested.

tasks.title: Replace WorkerStrategy dummy implementation with real delegation or safe decline
tasks.problem: WorkerStrategy currently hardcodes workers=[] and runtime_targets=[] and contains mock output logic. This must not be considered production behavior.
implementation_steps: Resolve real available workers from existing worker/agent registry instead of workers=[].
Resolve real runtime targets from runtime config instead of runtime_targets=[].
Delegate propose to selected worker adapter when selected.
Normalize worker output through LLMResponseNormalizer or worker-specific adapter.
Remove mock generated command/file output from production code.
If no worker is available, return declined no_worker_selected with reason_codes, not failed.
acceptance_criteria: WorkerStrategy with no workers returns declined no_worker_selected.
WorkerStrategy with mocked OpenCode worker can return PatchProposalArtifact or ExecutableProposal according to adapter output.
WorkerStrategy with Hermes in phase-1 proposal mode returns advisory/proposal but not mutation execute.
WorkerStrategy does not produce fake command output when no real worker exists.
ContextAccessPolicy is checked before sending context to remote/cloud worker.
done_definition: No TODO mock output remains in WorkerStrategy production path.
Worker fallback is real or safely declined.
Tests cover no worker, local worker, cloud-blocked worker and unsupported worker.

tasks.title: Persist and reload last_proposal consistently
tasks.problem: propose_task_step now calls persist_task_proposal_result when result is executable, but execute_task_step expects a certain last_proposal shape. This must be verified and normalized so propose and execute use the same schema.
implementation_steps: Inspect persist_task_proposal_result(...) output shape.

Ensure persisted last_proposal contains proposal_id, goal_id, task_id, strategy_id, command, tool_calls, required_tools, expected_artifacts, safety_flags, reason and metadata at the level expected by execute.
If existing persistence wraps fields under routing/worker_context/etc., add a canonical executable_proposal field or normalize during execute load.
Add schema version marker executable_proposal.v1.
acceptance_criteria: A proposal returned by ToolCallingLLMStrategy can be persisted and later executed without manual transformation.
A proposal returned by JsonSchemaLLMStrategy can be persisted and later executed without manual transformation.
Non-executable results are not persisted as last_proposal for execute.
Tests cover persisted shape roundtrip.
done_definition: Propose-to-execute contract is one stable shape.
No hidden shape mismatch between persistence service and execute path remains.

tasks.title: Add new-project E2E test with mocked Ollama/OpenAI-compatible provider
tasks.problem: The system needs an end-to-end test proving that ananta new-project really uses the LLM-default path and completes from artifacts/verification.
implementation_steps: Create mocked OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions provider fixture.
Configure default_provider=ollama or generic openai-compatible local provider in test config.
Make mocked provider return valid tool_calls or JSON schema response for Fibonacci project files.
Run propose step and assert selected_strategy is tool_calling_llm or json_schema_llm.
Run execute step and assert files are created.
Run artifact collection and verification.
Assert task completes from artifacts/verification, not final prose.
acceptance_criteria: Test fails if sgpt is called.
Test fails if deterministic_handler is selected before LLM for new_software_project.
Test fails if no ExecutableProposal is persisted.
Test fails if execute cannot reload last_proposal.
Test passes with mocked local LLM provider.
done_definition: new-project LLM-default path is proven by E2E test.
The test is deterministic and does not require internet or real paid provider.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for no sgpt, no raw prose execution and no infinite retry
tasks.problem: The original failure involved sgpt/free prose/parse failure/autopilot_no_executable_step. Regression tests must pin that down.
implementation_steps: Monkeypatch sgpt/run_sgpt_command to raise and prove propose path still works or fails explicitly without calling it.
Mock LLM returning plain natural language and assert result is advisory/needs_review, not executable.
Mock LLM returning malformed JSON and assert parse failure advances or terminates according to policy without infinite loop.
Assert retry counters are only used for retryable execution/artifact/verification failures, not endless parse repairs.
acceptance_criteria: sgpt is not called during default propose path.
Plain prose cannot become command/tool_calls.
Malformed JSON cannot become executable.
No infinite retry loop occurs after parse failure.
Reason codes are visible in result metadata.
done_definition: Original class of failure cannot silently reappear.
Tests document the intended safe failure modes.

tasks.title: Update todo.fixup-autopilot.json after finalization slice
tasks.problem: The larger consolidated TODO must be kept in sync after the focused runtime-finalization tasks are implemented.
implementation_steps: After implementing T001-T009, re-review actual code.
Update task statuses in todo.fixup-autopilot.json from todo/partial to done only where tests exist.
Reference this focused TODO as a follow-up slice or merge its final status into the main TODO.
Keep reviewed evidence and summary at the bottom of both files.
acceptance_criteria: Status summary matches actual code and tests.
No task is marked done without implementation and test evidence.
The final summary clearly states whether new-project LLM default is production-ready.
done_definition: TODO files are consistent and no longer overstate progress.
Final review summary is present.

critical_path.why: Execute currently has a likely runtime crash because of dataclass/Pydantic mismatch.
Fallback chain is currently truncated and flexible normalization/human review may not be reached.
new-project must actually require an LLM in the default path and fail explicitly if unavailable.
Propose and execute must share one stable persisted proposal shape.
Only E2E proves the intended new-project flow works.
Original failure class must be pinned by regression tests.

expected_final_state.new_project_default: LLM-required, local-first, Ollama/OpenAI-compatible capable, explicit failure if no LLM is available.
expected_final_state.proposal_contract: One validated ExecutableProposal contract from propose through persistence into execute.
expected_final_state.strategy_chain: Full configured chain is evaluated; max attempts do not truncate strategy ids.
expected_final_state.worker_fallback: Real worker delegation or safe declined result, no mock production output.
expected_final_state.completion: Artifact-first completion with tests and verification, not model prose.
expected_final_state.safety: No raw prose/Markdown execution, no sgpt default, no infinite parse retry.

review_summary_bottom.current_assessment: Architecture is close but not done. Real LLM strategies now exist, but execute validation and orchestrator fallback semantics are still blocking correctness.
review_summary_bottom.is_new_project_llm_default_correct_now: Not fully. It is policy-configured and partially implemented, but strict LLM-required behavior and E2E proof are still missing.
review_summary_bottom.most_important_next_fix: Fix ExecutableProposal validation and remove strategy_order slicing from the orchestrator.
